watchdog_ngos_searched: Democracy for the Arab World Now (DAWN)
adversarial_framing_check_finding: Lead Regional story is the US-Saudi civilian nuclear 123 agreement (signed 22-23 July 2026) alongside the Houthi Red Sea tanker attacks. Checked additional outlets for adversarial framing of Saudi Arabia on the same event: Guardian carried a Kenneth Roth (former HRW head) opinion piece framing the deal against the Kingdom's rights record; Foreign Policy and NYT framed it as triggering a Middle East nuclear arms race; Al Jazeera (Qatari state-funded, flagged) questioned the deal's security implications and normalisation strings. These are captured as Negative Articles items.
notes: Watchdog/NGO sweep run one-by-one across all nine organisations; every organisation had recent Saudi material (Amnesty executions/Filipino domestic workers, HRW abusive labour governance, Freedom House 'Not Free' 9/100, RSF/CPJ jailed-journalist tallies, CIVICUS 'Closed' rating, ALQST/DAWN/PEN advocacy) but none published a report dated inside the 24-hour coverage window; recorded as run, not as in-window hits. In-window negative coverage of Saudi Arabia is confined to critical/adversarial framing of the nuclear deal.
